title: Asteranthemum stellatum
taxonomy: Asteranthemum stellatum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification indicates that Asteranthemum stellatum is a type of flowering plant that produces daisy-like flowers. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants, including many common species such as daisies, sunflowers, and zinnias. Asteranthemum stellatum is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and is often found growing in rocky areas. By being a member of the Asteraceae family, Asteranthemum stellatum shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as composite flower heads.

intro: Asteranthemum stellatum, also known as Starflower, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, star-shaped flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Asteranthemum stellatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Asteranthemum stellatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia.
